pi_items.source: Web search of chemical supplier databases (Sigma-Aldrich, GFS Chemicals, Fisher Scientific); CAS registry confirmed across multiple authoritative chemical suppliers
Web search of chemical supplier databases confirms Ammonium Nitrate as the correct chemical name for CAS 6484-52-2

Web search for EPCRA Tier II thresholds found federal standards of 500 lbs (for EHS chemicals) or 10,000 lbs (for other hazardous chemicals). 1500 lbs does not appear in federal EPCRA regulations and may be a state-specific or synthetic threshold.

Analysis of epcra_tier2_data.xml: Database contains only 1 facility ('Green Valley Water Facility'). This facility does not contain Ammonium Nitrate. Python script searched all facilities for chemicals matching 'Ammonium Nitrate' with maxAmount > 1500 lbs, result: empty list.

XML parsing of epcra_tier2_data.xml reveals 1 facility: Green Valley Water Facility, located in Colchester, VT, Chittenden County. Contains Chlorine (7782-50-5, 20000 lbs) and Fluorosilic Acid (16961-83-4, 45000 lbs). No Ammonium Nitrate present.

privileged_info_summary: The EPCRA Tier II database (epcra_tier2_data.xml) contains only one facility: Green Valley Water Facility in Colchester, Vermont. This facility stores Chlorine (CAS 7782-50-5, 20,000 lbs) and Fluorosilic Acid (CAS 16961-83-4, 45,000 lbs). Ammonium Nitrate (CAS 6484-52-2) is not present in the database. The task's metadata claims five facilities (Agri-Grow Services, Industrial Blasting Co, Westside Garden Center, City Parks Dept, and Midwest Logistics) that do not exist in the actual data. The 1,500-pound threshold does not match standard federal EPCRA thresholds (500 lbs for EHS chemicals, 10,000 lbs for others). Critical missing components: setup_task.ps1 and export_result.ps1 hooks referenced in task.json do not exist in the task directory.
